falsificationAttempts.targetDescription: Queue-table + lease workers will reduce duplicate risk under concurrency
falsificationQueries: convex scheduler atomicity guarantees for queue enqueue paths
how action scheduling semantics can still produce duplication/drop windows
falsificationAttempts.falsificationAnalysis: If queue workers or enqueue paths involve actions, scheduling is non-atomic and action execution is at-most-once with no automatic retry. That creates concrete windows where a unit is partially progressed or lost unless additional exactly-once/outbox controls are implemented. Queue tables and leases alone do not prove duplicate-risk reduction.

falsificationAttempts.targetDescription: Queue architecture improves throughput predictability as concurrency grows
falsificationQueries: platform scheduling and transaction hard limits affecting queue workers
read/write/index limits under high queue churn
falsificationAttempts.falsificationAnalysis: Convex hard limits (scheduled fan-out caps, scheduled-job concurrency, transaction scan/write ceilings) and OCC contention behavior can cause non-linear degradation under queue hotspots. Therefore, predictability is not guaranteed by moving to queue tables; it depends on shard design, claim batching, and contention control that the hypothesis does not prove.
